Growth Insight
OpenAI reported that it achieved significant growth milestones in 2025, with its revenue reaching approximately $13.1 billion, exceeding its initial target of $10 billion. The company also projected its annual revenue to rise to over $25 billion in 2026, driven by strong demand for its AI products, including ChatGPT, which reached over 900 million weekly active users and more than 50 million paying subscribers. Additionally, OpenAI's enterprise business generated about $10 billion of its total revenue, highlighting the growing importance of corporate customers. The company secured a record-breaking $110 billion funding round led by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank, valuing it at $730 billion pre-money, which is expected to support its expansion and infrastructure needs.

Growth Insight
Polymarket achieved significant growth milestones in 2025, highlighted by a $2 billion investment from Intercontinental Exchange, which valued the company at approximately $9 billion. The platform's trading volume surged, reaching $7.66 billion in January 2026, up from $5.31 billion in December 2025, marking a 44% increase. Additionally, Polymarket reported a record monthly trading volume of $2.28 billion in December 2025. The company also expanded its user base, reaching 491,000 monthly active traders by December 2025, and saw a substantial increase in installs from 30,000 in January to 400,000 by December 2025. Furthermore, Polymarket's fee revenue reached approximately $787,000 in a single week, contributing to a sector-wide record in weekly fees.

Growth Insight
Sage reported a 14% year-on-year increase in pre-tax profit to GBP484 million for the year ended September 30, up from GBP426 million. The company also recorded a 16% increase in underlying operating profit to GBP600 million for the year to end-September, driven by both top-line growth and margin expansion. Additionally, Sage achieved a 10% year-on-year increase in first-quarter organic revenue to GBP647 million, supported by strong growth in its Sage Business Cloud and cloud-native offerings across multiple regions. The company expanded its operations by launching its Sage Intacct cloud financial management solution in Singapore and entered the Kenyan market, targeting mid-sized and SMB organizations. Furthermore, Sage raised $65 million in a Series C funding round led by Goldman Sachs Alternatives, bringing its total funding to $124 million to expand its AI-powered senior care platform.
